content: A structured, continuous system has been established for tracking LinkedIn posts, encompassing ideas, drafts, scheduling, and campaigns. This system integrates seamlessly with existing engineering workflows, treating marketing content as a first-class engineering task.
one_liner: New system established for structured, continuous LinkedIn content tracking integrated with engineering workflows.
source_context: Goal: Establish a structured, continuous system for tracking LinkedIn posts...

content: The existing GitHub Project board will be leveraged for tracking LinkedIn content pieces, using a `[Content]` prefix for issues. These content tasks will be sized as `Task` and categorized by `Node` (`portfolio` or `global_agent`) and `Impact` (`Revenue` or `Aesthetic`).
one_liner: GitHub Project board adopted for tracking LinkedIn content with specific issue tagging and categorization.
source_context: Proposed System - Global Ticketing (GitHub Board)... We will use your existing GitHub Project board.

content: A local filesystem structure under `registry/linkedin/` has been designed to manage LinkedIn content continuity. This includes dedicated directories for `ideas/` (raw Gold), `drafts/` (active refinement), and `posted/` (archive for performance analysis and repetition prevention).
one_liner: Local filesystem structure created (`registry/linkedin/`) for managing LinkedIn content lifecycle.
source_context: Proposed System - Local Storage & Continuity (`registry/linkedin/`)... We need a local filesystem structure...

content: The Allan Evans reply has been designated as the inaugural piece of content to be tracked within the new LinkedIn content system. It will be captured as a `[Content]` ticket on the GitHub board and its drafted text saved to `registry/linkedin/drafts/allan_evans_reply.md`, serving as a concrete example of the workflow.
one_liner: Allan Evans reply is the first content piece tracked by the new LinkedIn system, validating the workflow.
source_context: Goal... capture the Allan Evans reply as the first ticket.

content: Routine updates on task completion, including updating GitHub issue status, committing changes, running the session miner, pushing forensic docs, and updating the sprint plan.
source_context: Session Close - [x] Update GitHub issue status (#72)...
